name: Redis Cloud API
what_it_does: Managed Redis-as-a-service by Redis, Inc. — a blazing-fast in-memory data store for caching, session management, real-time messaging, and queues, with a REST management API and client library access.
use_cases: Caching API responses to reduce latency and downstream costs for agents
Storing agent conversation state and session data with TTL expiry
Pub/sub messaging between agent workers (real-time coordination)
Rate limiter counters and distributed locks for agent orchestration
Storing vector embeddings with Redis Vector Library (RediSearch)
not_for: Long-term persistent storage (Redis is in-memory; use a disk-backed DB for durable storage)
Compliance workloads requiring ACID guarantees across operations
best_when: An agent needs sub-millisecond cache lookups, pub/sub coordination across workers, or session state that should evaporate after a period.
avoid_when: You need full durability guarantees or complex querying — Redis is a complement to a primary database, not a replacement.

auth.notes: Redis Cloud management API uses API key + secret key pair. Data plane access uses Redis AUTH (password or ACL username/password). TLS client certificates supported for enhanced security.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free Essentials: up to 30MB storage, 30 connections, single-zone
pricing.paid_tiers: Essentials from $5/month; Pro from $30/month (multi-AZ, replication, persistence)
pricing.notes: Free tier is genuinely useful for development and small caches. Self-hosted Redis is free and open source.

performance.rate_limits: Determined by plan tier; no explicit API rate limit on data operations

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Redis error messages are terse but consistent and machine-readable (RESP protocol). Client library errors are well-typed.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Redis SETNX, SET NX/XX flags provide atomic conditional sets. Most commands are idempotent by design for their data structure.
known_agent_gotchas: TTL is set per-key; forgetting to set expiry on session keys causes memory growth
Pub/sub is fire-and-forget — messages are lost if no subscriber is connected
MULTI/EXEC transactions can't roll back on individual command errors
Redis Cloud management REST API is separate from the data plane (Redis protocol)
Connection limits per plan — agents that pool connections poorly may hit limits quickly

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS required for Redis Cloud. ACL support for fine-grained command/key permissions. Secrets stored via environment variables per Redis best practices. Redis Cloud offers VPC peering to avoid public internet exposure.
